pattern: Counting ordered ways to reach a target is a one-dimensional dynamic program where each amount sums the counts of the amounts reachable by subtracting a single number.
hints: Order matters here, so the same multiset in a different sequence counts separately, which hints at building counts up by the last number added.
Let ways(t) be the number of ordered sequences summing to t; reaching t means choosing some number n then any ordered way to make t minus n.
Fill ways from 0 up to target with ways(0) = 1, and for each t add ways(t - n) over every n that is at most t.

edge_inputs.description: only number larger than target yields zero
number equal to target counts once
all ones gives a single ordered way
target one with a matching number

stress.description: small distinct numbers and a moderate target so counts grow large
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randrange(1, 9)
    nums = sorted(set(rng.randrange(1, 12) for _ in range(k)))
    if not nums:
        nums = [1]
    target = rng.randrange(1, min(size, 1001))
    return [nums, target]
stress.note: small numbers keep many amounts reachable so the recurrence fires often
